# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build Commands

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      runs-on:
        description: Runner label (e.g., ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest)
        required: false
        default: ubuntu-latest
        type: string
      shell:
        description: Shell to run commands in (bash, pwsh, sh)
        required: false
        default: bash
        type: choice
        options:
          - bash
          - sh
          - pwsh
      working-directory:
        description: Working directory to run commands from
        required: false
        default: .
        type: string
      setup-commands:
        description: Optional setup commands (multiline). Runs before build-commands.
        required: false
        default: ""
        type: string
      build-commands:
        description: Build commands (multiline). Provide any shell commands to execute.
        required: true
        type: string
      post-commands:
        description: Optional post-build commands (multiline). Runs after build-commands.
        required: false
        default: ""
        type: string

jobs:
  build:
    name: Run Custom Build Commands
    runs-on: ${{ inputs.runs-on }}
    defaults:
      run:
        shell: ${{ inputs.shell }}
        working-directory: ${{ inputs.working-directory }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup commands
        if: ${{ inputs.setup-commands != '' }}
        run: ${{ inputs.setup-commands }}

      - name: Build commands
        run: ${{ inputs.build-commands }}

      - name: Post commands
        if: ${{ inputs.post-commands != '' }}
        run: ${{ inputs.post-commands }}
